text.description: The correct answer text. Null if answer was never confirmed or revealed.
text.title: Text

solver.description: Username of the first person to give the correct answer. Null if asker revealed without anyone getting it.
solver.title: Solver

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the winning answer message. For collaborative/answer_reveal cases this is the confirmation message timestamp. Null if unanswered.
timestamp.title: Timestamp

confirmed.description: Whether the asker explicitly confirmed the answer
confirmed.title: Confirmed

confirmation_text.description: The exact message the asker used to confirm (e.g. 'correct', 'Bingo', 'Yes!')
confirmation_text.title: Confirmation Text

is_collaborative.description: True when multiple participants together produced the full answer
is_collaborative.title: Is Collaborative

parts.description: For multi-part questions (X/Y/Z style). Null for single-answer questions.
parts.title: Parts

is_correct.description: True if this attempt was the winning answer. Null for non-attempt roles.
is_correct.title: Is Correct

media.description: Media attachments on this discussion message (e.g. an image posted as a hint). Null when unavailable or not applicable.
media.title: Media

reaction_counts.description: Total count per emoji across all thread messages. Keys are emoji characters, values are counts. Open-ended map.
reaction_counts.title: Reaction Counts

total_reactions.description: Sum of all reaction_counts values. Useful for sorting by engagement.
total_reactions.title: Total Reactions

categories.description: Derived category labels from a configurable emoji→category mapping (e.g. '😂'→'funny', '❤️'→'crowd_favourite'). New categories are added by updating the pipeline config, not this schema.
categories.title: Categories

type.description: Media type — image, video, audio, or document

url.description: URL of the hosted media file (e.g. CDN path, GitHub LFS URL). Null until the file is extracted from a WhatsApp backup and hosted.
url.title: Url

filename.description: Original filename from the WhatsApp backup (e.g. 'IMG-20260316-WA0007.jpg'). Null if unavailable.
filename.title: Filename

caption.description: Caption text attached to the media message, if any.
caption.title: Caption

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the question message
timestamp.title: Timestamp

text.description: Full question text, cleaned of WhatsApp artifacts
text.title: Text

asker.description: Username of the question poser (normalised, without leading ~)
asker.title: Asker

type.description: Question format type — describes the thinking required, independent of medium. Use has_media=True for image/video questions; they can be any type.

has_media.description: True if the question message included an image/video/audio (detected via 'image omitted' / 'video omitted' in the chat export). Remains True even when media[] is null — it means the file exists in the original chat but has not yet been extracted.
has_media.title: Has Media

media.description: Actual media attachments, populated when files are extracted from a WhatsApp backup. Null when unavailable (e.g. plain .txt export). has_media=True with media=null means: file exists but not yet extracted.
media.title: Media

topics.description: Topic categories. Empty list if not yet classified. A question can belong to multiple topics.
topics.title: Topics

message_timestamp.description: Timestamp of the reacted-to message. FK into question.timestamp (question itself) or discussion[].timestamp.
message_timestamp.title: Message Timestamp

score.description: Score at this point in the session
score.title: Score

question_number.description: Position of this question within the session (1-based)
question_number.title: Question Number

pair_index.description: Pair number within the source file (1-based)
pair_index.title: Pair Index

wrong_attempts.description: Number of incorrect attempts before the correct answer
wrong_attempts.title: Wrong Attempts

hints_given.description: Number of hints/nudges the asker gave
hints_given.title: Hints Given

time_to_answer_seconds.description: Seconds from question timestamp to first correct answer. Null if unanswered.
time_to_answer_seconds.title: Time To Answer Seconds

unique_participants.description: Number of distinct users who attempted an answer
unique_participants.title: Unique Participants

difficulty.description: Heuristic: easy=0 wrong attempts; medium=1–3; hard=4+. Or LLM-assigned.

description: A single extracted Q&A pair from the KVizzing WhatsApp group.

id.description: Unique identifier. Format: YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS (e.g. '2025-09-23-192540'). If two questions share the same second, a digit suffix is appended: '2025-09-23-1925402', '2025-09-23-1925403'.
id.title: Id

discussion.description: Ordered list of all relevant messages in the Q&A thread
discussion.title: Discussion

extraction_confidence.description: Confidence that this is a genuine Q&A pair with a known answer

session.description: Null for ad-hoc questions; populated for QM-hosted quiz sessions

scores_after.description: Scores announced by the quizmaster immediately after this question, if present. Null if no score announcement followed this question. Only populated for session questions — never for ad-hoc questions.
scores_after.title: Scores After

reactions.description: Optional enrichment from WhatsApp SQLite DB (iOS: ChatStorage.sqlite; Android: msgstore.db). Null when unavailable.
reactions.title: Reactions

highlights.description: Optional enrichment derived from reactions[]. Null when reactions unavailable.

title: KVizzingQuestion
